actionLibraryList.title: Aerobic Skiing (bent arms)
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand in the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees and hips slightly bent, and lean forward slightly with your hands gripping the handles.
2.Keep your arms bent, initiating the pull by engaging your core. Swing your arms back past your thighs.
3.Bend and extend the knees in a fluid motion, driving the handles downward by hinging at the hips. Exhale as you pull, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the slider to the highest position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, hinge at your hips with a slight bend in your knees, and grip the handles with both hands.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Knees bend and extend in a fluid motion, while your hips sit back and down.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your arms bent and use your body's explosive power to pull the handles down.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Engage your core throughout the movement. Exhale as you pull, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: As you pull the handles down to your hips, engage your back and lower body muscles.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep your back straight.\n2.Hold the ski handles with both hands, keeping them no higher than shoulder level.\n3.Bend your elbows and pull the ropes down to your sides with force.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750671815181.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep your arms close to your body and maintain good balance throughout.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750671818931.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Engage your core throughout the movement. Exhale while pulling, inhale when returning to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: As you pull the handles down toward your hips, activate your back and lower body muscles.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your arms bent and use your body's explosive power to pull the handles down.

actionLibraryList.title: Rolling Shrugs
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, arms relaxed.
2.Lead the movement with your shoulder blades, circling up, back, down, and forward.
3.Complete a full circle, then reverse direction.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750400914511.jpg","context":"1.Engage your core.\n2.Lead the movement with your shoulder blades."}]
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep the torso stable, avoiding swinging or using momentum.

actionLibraryList.title: Shoulder Stretch
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Maintain a neutral spine; avoid leaning forward or backward.
2.Keep shoulders relaxed and down; avoid shrugging.
3.Perform the stretch slowly and controlled; do not use momentum.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Bring your arm across your body and hold it straight.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Use your other arm to pull your elbow toward your chest.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: You should feel a strong and intense stretch in your posterior deltoid.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Bring one arm across your body and hold it straight.\n2.Pull your elbow toward your chest with the other arm to deepen the stretch.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748513898963.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: You should feel your posterior deltoid getting a strong and intense stretch.

actionLibraryList.title: Pulse Squat to Alternating Hip Abduction
actionLibraryList.context:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and engage your core. Lower into a half squat, keeping your thighs at about a 45-degree angle to the floor. Keep your knees stable, hips back, and chest lifted—don’t round your back.
While holding the half squat, bend one knee and lift the leg out to the side into a hip abduction. Control the motion and avoid leaning or rotating your pelvis. Return slowly, then repeat on the other side. Keep your core tight and movements smooth throughout.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1751443530145.jpg","context":"1.The leg is above horizontal"},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1751443534988.jpg","context":"1.Keep the supporting knee in line with the toes"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you exert, inhale on the return.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: The glutes and legs should be actively engaged.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Maintain core stability and smooth movement.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with your feet hip-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
2.Hold the barbell and place it across your upper traps, just below the neck.
3.Keeping your chest up, drive your hips back.
4.Allow your knees to move forward in the direction your toes are pointing.
5.Aim to achieve 90 degrees at both knees, with your eyes forward.
6.Driving through your heels, lift your body back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulleys to the footboard end with the barbell attachment.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Squat until your thighs are parallel to or slightly below the floor.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your chin up, chest out, back straight, core tight, and your toes in line with your knees.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Keep your breath steady. Inhale as you descend, and exhale as you ascend.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Initiate the ascending movement by engaging your glutes.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Load the barbell onto your traps and shoulders.
2.Keep your back straight and your glutes engaged.
3.Slightly bend your knees.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750230418406.jpg"},{"context":"1.Lower into a squat with an upright back until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
2.Align your knees and toes, and avoid letting your knees cave in.
3.Slightly rotate your feet outward.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750230423096.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you rise, inhale as you lower.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your chin up, chest open, back straight, core tight, and your toes in line with your knees.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than hip-width.
2.Keep your shoulders back, core engaged, and knees slightly bent.
3.Begin driving your hips backward.
4.Avoid excessive knee bending or losing proper posture in your upper body.
5.Continue bending, feeling the stretch in your hamstrings.
6.Pause briefly at the bottom position, then drive your hips forward to return to standing.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulleys to the floor with the barbell attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Start by establishing a neutral spine while standing.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Hinge backward, keeping your back straight, and feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Maintain smooth breathing throughout. Inhale as you lower yourself, exhale as you rise.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Hinge at your hips to fully stretch your hamstrings.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Relax your shoulders.
2.Keep your back straight and glutes engaged.
3.Slightly bend your knees.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749439326943.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep your back straight.
2.Slightly bend your knees, keeping the barbell close to your body.
3.Feet hip-width apart.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749439330202.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale on the way up, inhale on the way down.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Feel a full stretch in your hamstrings as you hinge forward.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your knees only slightly bent.

actionLibraryList.title: Kneeling Alternating Lat Pulldown
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Kneel on the platform with core engaged and back straight.
2.Hold the handles with palms facing forward.
3.Pull the handles down along your sides toward your chest, keeping elbows close and pointing down.
4.Feel your back muscles contract, then slowly return and repeat on the other side.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749004666614.jpg","context":"1.Hold the handles with palms facing forward."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749004669769.jpg","context":"1.Keep your elbows close to your body and pull them downward."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you pull down, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your back muscles to generate the force.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Throughout the movement, avoid shrugging your shoulders, using body momentum, or compensating with other muscles.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Dead Bug
actionLibraryList.context: Lie supine on the machine, grasp the handles with your arms fully extended straight up, palms facing each other or down. Bend your knees to 90 degrees, with your knees positioned directly above your hips. Keep your arms stable and pointing downward, then alternate extending one leg forward and downward while keeping the other leg in place. Control the return to the starting position, then switch sides, mimicking the "dead bug" movement.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749453367842.jpg","context":"1.Keep your arms straight and extended vertically upward, with your palms facing each other or facing downward.\n2.Bend your knees to 90 degrees, with your knees positioned directly above your hips."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749453371890.jpg","context":"1.Alternately extend the opposite leg straight forward and downward."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you lower your leg, inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: You should feel a strong contraction in your core.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid arching your lower back off the floor, keep your pelvis stable, and ensure your arms don't drive the movement.
